title: Memory inference fails when output register has initial value
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

(Xilinx BRAMs, for example, support initial values on RAMs outputs)

`./yosys -p "synth -run coarse; memory_bram -rules +/xilinx/brams.txt; stat" ram2.v -f "verilog -DBUG"`

```
module reg_file(input wire clk,
                input wire wr_en,
                input wire [9:0] w_addr,
                input wire [9:0] r_addr,
                input wire [17:0] w_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data2);

  reg [17:0] ram [0:1023];
`ifdef BUG
  initial r_data <= {18{1'b0}};
`endif

  always @(posedge clk)
  begin
    r_data <= ram[r_addr];
  end

endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

Successful inference (when `-DBUG` is omitted), and obviously with the correct `(* init *)` attributes.

```
   Number of cells:                 11
     $__XILINX_RAMB18_SDP            2
     $dff                            2
     $eq                             2
     $mux                            4
     $pmux                           1
```

## Actual behavior

Failed inference
```
   Number of cells:                  2
     $dff                            1
     $mem                            1
```
code_blocks:
module reg_file(input wire clk,
                input wire wr_en,
                input wire [9:0] w_addr,
                input wire [9:0] r_addr,
                input wire [17:0] w_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data,
                output reg [17:0] r_data2);

  reg [17:0] ram [0:1023];
`ifdef BUG
  initial r_data <= {18{1'b0}};
`endif

  always @(posedge clk)
  begin
    r_data <= ram[r_addr];
  end

endmodule
